---
title: "Project Home"
---

# About The Project

![](Breast%20vs%20Cervical.png)

This project explores disparities in breast and cervical cancer outcomes, with a focus on how access to healthcare influences screening and early detection. Using national data ([Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System)](https://www.cdc.gov/brfss/index.html), I examine how factors like insurance, routine checkups, and having a personal doctor impact whether women receive life-saving screenings. 

# Why This Matters

Breast and cervical cancers are among the most common cancers affecting women worldwide. While both are highly treatable, and in some cases preventable, when detected early, thousands of women still die each year due to delayed diagnosis and limited access to care. Additonally, many women are not aware of when or how to get screened. Gaps in health literacy and access to clear, culturally relevant information contribute to delayed diagnosis and missed prevention opportunities. These knowledge gaps, combined with structural barriers, continue to drive preventable deaths.

# Who This Affects

These disparities disproportionately impact:

-   Women in low-income communities

-   Uninsured or underinsured individuals

-   Racial/ethnic minority populations

-   Women with limited access to routine healthcare/ education

# Key Statistics

-   2.3 million new breast cancer cases globally each year [🔗](https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/breast-cancer#:~:text=Treatment%20is%20based%20on%20the,of%20management%20as%20for%20women.)

-   \~670,000 breast cancer deaths annually [🔗](https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/breast-cancer#:~:text=Treatment%20is%20based%20on%20the,of%20management%20as%20for%20women.)

-   \~660,000 cervical cancer cases worldwide [🔗](https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/cervical-cancer)

-   \~350,000 cervical cancer deaths annually [🔗](https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/cervical-cancer)

-   Nearly **4,000 U.S. women die from cervical cancer each year** [🔗](https://www.cdc.gov/hpv/about/cancers-caused-by-hpv.html#:~:text=Number%20of%20Cancers%20Caused%20by,get%20cancers%20caused%20by%20HPV.)

    -   Despite cervical cancer being considered one of the most preventable cancers
